---
title: "Client Report - Elliot"
subtitle: "Course DS 250"
author: "Ash Uchida"
format:
  html:
    self-contained: true
    page-layout: full
    title-block-banner: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    toc-location: body
    number-sections: false
    html-math-method: katex
    code-fold: true
    code-summary: "Show the code"
    code-overflow: wrap
    code-copy: hover
    code-tools:
        source: false
        toggle: true
        caption: See code
execute: 
  warning: false
    
---


```{python}
import polars as pl
import numpy as np
from lets_plot import *

LetsPlot.setup_html(isolated_frame=True)

```

```{python}
df = pl.read_csv("/Users/ash/Documents/Data-Science-portfolio/Cleansing_Projects/names_year_csv.csv")
```

# Famous movies with the name Elliot
E.T which released 1982

Get a Life which released 1990, although the charcaters name wasn't Elliot, the actor Chris Elliott was a big hit.
 
Mr. Robot which released it's finale in 2019

```{python}
elliot = df.filter(pl.col('name') == 'Elliot')

(
  ggplot(elliot, aes(x='year', y='Total'))
  +geom_line(aes(color='name'),size=1,)
  +scale_color_manual(values={'Elliot':'blue'})

  +scale_x_continuous(format='d')
  +geom_vline(xintercept=1982, color="red", linetype='dashed')
  +geom_vline(xintercept=1990, color="red", linetype='dashed')
  +geom_vline(xintercept=2019, color="red", linetype='dashed')

  +geom_text(x=1965, y=3000, label="E.T released (1982)")
  +geom_text(x=1979, y=2000, label="Get a life(1990)")
  +geom_text(x=2005, y=250, label="Mr.Robot(2019)")
  
  +labs(title="Elliot...What?")
  +theme(legend_position='right')

)

```
